original_image_tags: A scan of the back of a document, which is mostly blank, off-white paper. It has two large, black circular hole-punch marks at the top edge and some faint creasing. In the bottom left corner, there is a handwritten number in pencil or faded ink. In the bottom right quadrant, there is a blue ink stamp applied upside down relative to the page's primary orientation.
A blue ink stamp, oriented upside down, in the lower right quadrant of the page. The stamp text is arranged in four lines.
In the bottom left corner, a handwritten number is visible, written in a faint, dark medium (pencil or faded ink). The number appears to be "9389".

elements.description: One stamped ink mark located in the lower right quadrant of the page. The stamp is oriented upside down relative to the page. It contains multiple lines of text in a sans-serif typeface. The text reads, from top to bottom as oriented on the stamp: 'MR. JONES', 'JUL 8 1:50 PM '47', 'RECEIVED', 'F B I', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE'.
Two circular punch holes are present along the top edge of the page, positioned in the upper left and upper center.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific stamp text ('MR. JONES', 'JUL 8 1:50 PM '47', etc.) that Description A does not mention and describes as only four lines of text, creating a direct contradiction in the quoted content of the same visual element.
